urn:contentItem:6JJW-8BN3-S333-S070-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ ADDING TO GLOBAL TOP FOUR EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL TEAM, PILLSBURY WELCOMES PARTNER JOHN ROCKWELL IN SILICON VALLEY

Continuing the expansion of Pillsbury's global Emerging Companies & Venture Capital practice, the firm has welcomed another well-known partner to the team: John Rockwell in Silicon Valley. Rockwell, who comes to Pillsbury from WilmerHale, brings valuable cross-border experience in Latin America and even greater depth to a practice that was recently ranked by LSEG as among the four most active in the world for the representation of company-side venture capital financings.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY PREVAILS IN WINERY DISPUTE AFTER FIVE-DAY JURY TRIAL

Ateam of Pillsbury lawyers led by Austin-based Litigation partner Casey Low prevailed in a five-day jury trial in favor of client Fred Schrader, the founder of the highly acclaimed Schrader Cellars winery, and the winery's current owner.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ KING & SPALDING STEPHEN VAUGHN NAMED ONE OF D.C.S 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE BY WASHINGTONIAN MAGAZINE

King &Spalding Partner Stephen Vaughn has been named one of Washington, D.C.'s 500 Most Influential People by Washingtonian magazine.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ SIDLEY REBALANCES ETF DISPUTE IN DELAWARE SUPREME COURT WIN

Sidley secured a major victory on behalf of Benchmark Investments LLC in an appeal before the Delaware Supreme Court. On April 30, 2026, the Court unanimously reversed an adverse summary judgement decision in a contract dispute with Pacer Advisors concerning termination rights under an exchange-traded fund (ETF) services agreement.

Source: Company Website

+ SIDLEY REPRESENTS S3 CAPITAL IN US$1.3 BILLION REAL ESTATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Sidley represented S3 Capital Advisors LLC (S3), a New York-based real estate credit firm, in the formation and closing of S3 LB Real Estate Credit III (the Fund) and related co-investment accounts, which secured approximately US$1.3 billion in commitments.

Source: Company Website

+ SIDLEY ADVISES ON BROOKFIELD INDIA REIT AND POONAWALLA FINCORP QUALIFIED INSTITUTIONS PLACEMENTS

Sidley has advised on two significant qualified institutions placements (QIPs) in India, for Brookfield India Real Estate Trust (Brookfield India REIT) and Poonawalla Fincorp, together raising over ₹5,100 crore (US$550 million).

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP SOCIAL LINKS: PAPERS, PLEASE

A federal court's decision blocking key provisions of Arkansas' social media age-verification law rests on familiar constitutional ground.

Source: Company Website

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP HOW NEW E-EVIDENCE RULES WILL AFFECT EU-US DATA TRANSFERS

In an article recently authored for Law360, Alex van der Wolk, chair of Morrison Foerster's European Data, Cyber + Privacy practice, and Dan Alam, Data, Cyber + Privacy associate, looked back at how cross-border requests from law enforcement have historically been viewed within the EU, and how new e-evidence regulation will change that approach, while also discussing the challenges that EU and U.S. businesses will face navigating conflicting obligations across the two continents.

Source: Company Website

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP SHOT ACROSS THE BOW OR A FATAL STRIKE: NEW SENATE BILL WOULD END 8(A) AND WOMEN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESS PREFERENCES

For all its controversy, complications, successes, and failures, the Small Business Administration's 8(a) Business Development Program (the "8(a) Program") has served as an access point for socially and economically disadvantaged entrepreneurs to join the federal marketplace for more than 50 years, while the Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Federal Contract Program has provided set-aside authority for women business owners since 2011.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [5 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS SEASONED FINANCE AND RESTRUCTURING SHAREHOLDER GERARD C. MARTIN IN CHICAGO

CHICAGO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added secured finance attorney Gerard C. Martin to its Chicago office as a shareholder in the firm's Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. He joins the firm from Reed Smith LLP.

Martin brings deep experience across the full spectrum of financing transactions and debt restructurings. He represents lenders, borrowers, sponsors, and investors in a broad range of U.S. and international financing transactions, including cash flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, bridge facilities, recapitalizations, and complex intercreditor arrangements, including unitranche and multitranche structures. He regularly advises on large, syndicated credit facilities and cross-border and multicurrency financings. Martin also maintains an active equipment and aviation finance practice, representing parties in aircraft and engine financings, leveraged leases, rolling stock transactions, and project finance matters. He works with clients across a wide range of industries, including manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care, and financial services, among others.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISED REX CONCEPTS AND MCWIN ON THE INITIAL PUBLIC OFFERING AND LISTING ON THE WARSAW STOCK EXCHANGE

WARSAW -7 May 2026- Greenberg Traurig advised Rex Concepts S.A. (the "Company"), the master franchisee of Burger King and Popeyes in Poland, Czechia and Romania, as well as Rex Invest CEE - a company in the McWin group - as the selling shareholder, on the initial public offering (IPO) of the Company's shares and the admission of the shares to trading on the regulated market operated by the Warsaw Stock Exchange.

The total value of the offering amounted to approximately PLN 497.8 million, with a final offer price set at PLN 14 per share. The offering comprised 32 million newly issued and 3,555,555 existing shares offered by Rex Invest CEE in connection with the stabilisation structure. Gross proceeds from the new issue amounted to approximately PLN 448 million.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISES SELLER ON HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER TRANSACTION IN JAPAN

TOKYO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented the seller in the disposition of a stabilized hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The transaction is among the largest asset‑level sales of a fully leased, operational hyperscale data center in the Japanese market to date.

The approximately 40‑megawatt facility is leased to a single U.S. hyperscale cloud provider. The seller is a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a U.S. institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company, and the buyer is a consortium comprising Singapore‑listed CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, and a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company, which acquired the remaining 51%.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG EXPANDS GLOBAL HOSPITALITY PRACTICE WITH STRATEGIC HIRES IN PHOENIX AND MEXICO

NEW YORK - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has strengthened its market-leading Real Estate and Hospitality practices with the addition of two seasoned cross-border attorneys focused on hospitality, real estate, and international matters. Carlos A. Sugich joins as co-chair of the Americas Hospitality Group in Phoenix and Mexico, and Carlos Freaner joins as a shareholder in Mexico. They arrive from Snell & Wilmer.

Building on the strength of Greenberg Traurig's 70-plus-attorney Mexico City office, the firm is expanding its cross-border capabilities in Mexico and key hospitality markets with a new presence in Los Cabos, where Sugich will serve as managing shareholder. He led the launch of his prior firm's Los Cabos office nearly two decades ago.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP MATHEW ROSENGART NAMED AMONG THE 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE IN LOS ANGELES BY THE LOS ANGELES BUSINESS JOURNAL FOR FIFTH CONSECUTIVE YEAR

LOS ANGELES - May 7, 2026 - Mathew S. Rosengart, co-chair of the Media & Entertainment Litigation Practice of global law firmGreenberg Traurig, LLP, was again named to the Los Angeles Business Journal's "LA500" list. According to the publication, the list honors the 500 most influential people in Los Angeles, based on their varied achievements in the community.

Rosengart, a former Justice Department trial attorney, has been heralded as "Hollywood's King of Litigators" by Variety, called a "globally recognized legal eagle" by The Hollywood Reporter, acclaimed as a "household name" by Billboard and the Los Angeles Business Journal, which referred to him as a "trusted counsellor" who is "widely recognized as one of the nation's preeminent litigators," and recognized as a "high powered" lawyer by The New York Times. He has twice been honored by Forbes in its list of the Top 200 lawyers in the United States, which referred to him as a "revered" litigator and, according to the publication, recognizes the "finest in the field," whose "integrity," "expertise, passion, and purpose set them apart" as "the best in the business."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PLC [4 Press Releases]

+ GOODWIN GUIDES PHARMAKON ADVISORS ON $250 MILLION DEBT FACILITY WITH ZENAS BIOPHARMA

The Intellectual Property team advised Pharmakon Advisors on entering into a five-year, up to $250 million senior secured debt facility with Zenas BioPharma, who are developing a bifunctional monoclonal antibody drug product for the treatment of IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD).

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES HUMANS& IN RAISING OVER $500 MILLION AT $4.5 BILLION VALUATION IN SEED ROUND

The Technology team guided humans& in raising over $500 million in a seed financing round, which values the company at approximately $4.5 billion. The round was led by SV Angel and co-founder Georges Harik, while Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, and Alphabet's VC arm, GV, among other venture capital investors, also participated.

Source : Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES EVANTIC CAPITAL IN PARTICIPATION IN SYNTHESIAS $200 MILLION SERIES E

The Technology team advised Evantic Capital in its participation in the $200 million Series E funding round for Synthesia, the AI video platform transforming how organizations create and share knowledge.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. American Lawyer recognized the win in its Litigators of the Week column.

Source : Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-91BK-00000-00Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) Sets Date for 2026 Annual Stockholders Meeting in Fremont2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisEnphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) Sets Date for 2026 Annual Stockholders Meeting in Fremont

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 15:02 EDT

Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH), a global energy technology company, will hold its Annual Meeting of Stockholders on May 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. Pacific Time, at the company’s headquarters located at 47281 Bayside Parkway, Fremont, California. The announcement, dated May 5, 2026, notes that voting procedures and participation for stockholders are described in the company’s 2026 Proxy Statement filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Following this event, the formal portion of the meeting will be followed by a presentation by the company’s President and CEO Badri Kothandaraman at approximately 9:30 a.m. Pacific Time. The presentation will be accessible via internet and telephone, with registration required for online attendance. This portion of the program is expected to provide insights into the company’s strategic direction and performance. Enphase Energy has deployed over 5.2 million Enphase-based systems in more than 165 countries, illustrating its reach in the renewable energy sector. Investors will hear updates on product developments and market expansions discussed during the meeting. For further information about Enphase Energy's 2026 Annual Meeting and CEO presentation, stakeholders are encouraged to visit the company’s Investor Relations website.

ENPHASE ENERGY INC. (ENPH) Stock Dashboard [traded in US Dollars, USD] Wed, May 06

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 ENPHASE ENERGY ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Enphase Energy Activities

Activities

Enphase Energy Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company headquartered in Fremont, California, is a leading innovator in the renewable energy sector. The company specializes in designing, developing, and manufacturing advanced microinverter systems, solar energy storage solutions, and intelligent software platforms that optimize energy production and consumption. At its core, Enphase focuses on enhancing the efficiency and reliability of solar power systems by converting direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) at the module level, reducing risks associated with traditional string inverters. The fastest-growing segments include its IQ-series microinverters and Encharge battery storage systems, which have seen rapid adoption in residential and commercial markets due to increasing demand for grid-independent energy solutions and supportive renewable policies. This growth is fueled by technological advancements, such as integrated app-based monitoring and seamless compatibility with emerging smart home ecosystems, positioning Enphase as a key driver in the global transition to sustainable energy. It is the NASDAQ's 8th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Agriculture Agency for Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-M353-SG5M-91RC-00000-00Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Agriculture Agency for Lyndon Renewable Energy Project2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisPRESS RELEASESVermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Agriculture Agency for Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

IRVINE, CA, May 07, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: CETY) (“CETY” or the “Company”), a clean energy technology and solutions provider focused on converting waste and heat into power and fuels, today announced that its affiliate, Vermont Renewable Gas (VRG), has entered into a Stipulation (MOU) with the Vermont Agency of Agriculture, Food and Markets, establishing comprehensive agricultural and soil protection requirements for its proposed 2.2 MW renewable energy facility in Lyndon.

Key Step in the Approval ProcessThis MOU, filed with the Vermont Public Utility Commission, reflects a full resolution of agricultural impacts and sets detailed conditions governing soil preservation, feedstock sourcing, and biochar quality for the Lyndon project. It represents a significant milestone in the VRG project’s review under Vermont’s Section 248 process as Vermont Public Utility Commission continues its review. Through compliance with these conditions, the Project is expected to avoid undue adverse impact on the primary agricultural soils. These soils are a critical natural resource that support long-term food production, water retention, ecosystem stability, and sustainable agricultural development.

Protecting Vermont Agricultural SoilsUnder the terms of the agreement:

Primary agricultural soils (PAS) impacted by construction will be preserved, managed, and fully restored in accordance with Vermont agricultural standards

Soil structure and integrity must be maintained through pre- and post-construction testing, with strict limits on compaction

Any disturbed soils must be reclaimed and returned to productive agricultural condition, even after project decommissioning

These requirements ensure that agricultural lands remain viable for future use and are protected throughout the life of the project.

Strict Biochar Safety and Environmental ControlsThe Project will also be subject to robust testing and operational safeguards, including:

Comprehensive testing of biochar for PFAS, heavy metals, dioxins, PCBs, and other contaminants

Ongoing monitoring of product quality and operating conditions

Prohibition on distribution of biochar if any contaminants exceed regulatory thresholds

Detailed controls on acceptable feedstock, including exclusion of treated wood, plastics, and contaminated materials

The agreement establishes one of the most rigorous frameworks for biochar quality and agricultural safety in the region.

Transparency and AccountabilityVRG will be required to:

Maintain traceability of feedstock sources

Notify regulators of any material changes in operations or sourcing

Provide regular testing data and allow regulatory oversight

“These conditions ensure a high standard of accountability,” said Kam Mahdi, CEO. “We are committed to ensuring that this project operates safely, protects Vermont’s agricultural resources, and meets the expectations of regulators and the community.”

About Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY)

Headquartered in Irvine, California, Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY) is a rising leader in the zero-emission revolution by offering eco-friendly green energy solutions, clean energy fuels and alternative electric power for small and mid-sized projects in North America, Europe, and Asia. CETY also holds a minority ownership interest in, and is affiliated with Vermont renewable Gas LLC. We deliver power from heat and biomass with zero emission and low cost. The Company's principal products are Waste Heat Recovery Solutions using our patented Clean CycleTM generator to create electricity. Waste to Energy Solutions convert waste products created in manufacturing, agriculture, wastewater treatment plants and other industries to electricity and BioChar. Engineering, Consulting and Project Management Solutions provide expertise and experience in developing clean energy projects for municipal and industrial customers and Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) companies.

CETY's common stock is currently traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “CETY.” For more information, visit www.cetyinc.com.

Follow CETY on our social media channels: Twitter | LinkedIn | Facebook

About Vermont Renewable GasVermont Renewable Gas, LLC is a developer of renewable energy infrastructure focused on converting underutilized organic resources into sustainable power solutions. The Company is committed to advancing projects that meet stringent environmental standards while supporting local economies and energy resilience.

This summary should be read in conjunction with the Company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended September 30, 2025 and other periodic filings made pursuant to Section 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, which contain, among other matters, risk factors and financial footnotes as well as a discussions of our business, operations and financial matters located on the website of the Securities and Exchange Commission at www.sec.gov.

Because forward-looking statements relate to the future, they are subject to inherent uncertainties, risks and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict and many of which are outside of the Company's control. Therefore, you should not rely on any of these forward-looking statements. value.Title: WEEKLY REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-YGX3-RTWH-J4YM-00000-00WEEKLY REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisWEEKLY REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJT-YG93-S384-J3SJ-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-02F3-S13P-4390-00000-00Past Week: PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) falls for a fourth consecutive week, a four-week fall of 12%; -$2.21 [-12.0%]2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisPast Week: PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) falls for a fourth consecutive week, a four-week fall of 12%; -$2.21 [-12.0%]

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG $16.18), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has fallen 19.0c (or 1.2%) on high volume to close at $16.18. The stock fell for a fourth consecutive week on Wednesday bringing its a four-week fall to $2.21 or 12.0%. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 582.3 points (or 8.6%) in the four weeks, this represented a relative price change of -20.6%.

In the past four weeks the market cap has plunged by $4.9 billion to $35.6 billion.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

SEC Form 4-Insider Selling

Tuesday April 28

PG&E (NYSE:PCG) Chief Executive Officer and Director Patricia K. Poppe sold 31,250 shares worth $512,188 on April 28. The selling price was $16.39. The shares hit a four-month low on the day.

Wednesday's price drop

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) drops to three-month low

SAN FRANCISCO, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - PG&E Corporation's (NYSE: PCG $16.18) stock price traded at its 90-day low of $16.0. The last time the price hit that low level was on Thursday, Feb 05, 2026. PG&E Corporation's stock price decreased 15.50c (0.9%) from Tuesday's close of $16.18. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 102.5 points (1.4%) in the day, the relative price change was -2.4%. There were 14,836,634 shares worth $240 million traded today; the volume was 0.6 times the average daily trading volume of 23 million shares.

SECTION 8 CORPORATE PROFILE

8.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.
