value.Title: -ESS Announces Successful Commissioning of Long-Duration Iron Flow Battery Systems at Turlock Irrigation District Solar-Over-Canal Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0X03-S69R-W4J3-00000-00-ESS Announces Successful Commissioning of Long-Duration Iron Flow Battery Systems at Turlock Irrigation District Solar-Over-Canal Project2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexis-ESS Announces Successful Commissioning of Long-Duration Iron Flow Battery Systems at Turlock Irrigation District Solar-Over-Canal Project

May 6, 2026

Release date- 05052026 - WILSONVILLE - ESS Tech, Inc. (NYSE: GWH) ('ESS' or the 'Company'), a leading manufacturer of long-duration iron flow energy storage systems ('LDES') for commercial and utility-scale applications, today announced the successful commissioning of two ESS iron flow battery systems at Turlock Irrigation District ('TID') in California's Central Valley.



The project pairs ESS iron flow battery technology with solar panels installed above active irrigation canals, an innovative configuration designed to generate renewable electricity while helping reduce water evaporation. ESS believes the project demonstrates the ability of long-duration iron flow battery technology to support critical infrastructure applications where reliability, safety and flexible energy dispatch are important.

'The successful commissioning of this project is an important milestone for ESS and a strong demonstration of our iron flow battery technology in a real-world infrastructure application,' said Drew Buckley, Chief Executive Officer of ESS Tech. 'We are proud to support Turlock Irrigation District on this innovative project and believe it highlights the potential for long-duration energy storage to play a valuable role in helping customers manage renewable energy, strengthen resilience and support broader resource conservation goals.'

The solar-over-canal configuration is drawing growing interest as water agencies and utilities look for ways to address both energy and water challenges. By combining renewable generation with long-duration storage, the TID project is designed to improve the usability of solar power produced at the site while also supporting water conservation objectives.

ESS's iron flow battery technology uses iron, salt and water as its primary materials and is designed to provide safe, long-duration energy storage for stationary applications. The collaboration underscores ESS's commitment to safe and sustainable energy infrastructure.

About ESS Tech, Inc.

ESS (NYSE: GWH) is the leading manufacturer of long-duration iron flow energy storage solutions. ESS was established in 2011 with a mission to accelerate decarbonization safely and sustainably through longer lasting energy storage. Using easy-to-source iron, salt, and water, ESS iron flow technology enables energy security, reliability and resilience. We build flexible storage solutions that allow our customers to meet increasing energy demand without power disruptions and maximize the value potential of excess energy. For more information visit www.essinc.com.

Cautionary Language on Forward-Looking Statements

This communication contains forward-looking statements (including within the meaning of Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended) concerning the Company and other matters that involve substantial risks and uncertainties. These statements may discuss the management team's goals, beliefs, hopes, intentions and expectations as to future plans, trends, events, results of operations and financial condition, or otherwise, based on current beliefs of the management of the Company, as well as assumptions made by, and information currently available to, the Company's management. These forward-looking statements can be identified by the use of forward-looking terminology, including the words 'anticipate,' 'believe,' 'continue,' 'could,' 'estimate,' 'expect,' 'intends,' 'may,' 'might,' 'plan,' 'possible,' 'potential,' 'predict,' 'project,' 'should,' 'will,' 'would,' or, in each case, their negative or other variations or comparable terminology may identify forward-looking statements, but the absence of these words does not mean that a statement is not forward-looking. These forward-looking statements, which are subject to risks, uncertainties and assumptions about us, may include our anticipated growth strategies and anticipated trends in our business. Examples of forward-looking statements include, among others, statements pertaining to market opportunities for ESS' products, pace of commercial activity, and relationships with customers. These forward-looking statements are based on ESS' current expectations and beliefs concerning future developments and their potential effects on ESS. Many factors could cause actual future events to differ materially from the forward-looking statements in this communication. There can be no assurance that the future developments affecting ESS will be those that we have anticipated. These forward-looking statements involve a number of risks, uncertainties (some of which are beyond ESS control) or other assumptions that may cause actual results or performance to be materially different from those expressed or implied by these forward-looking statements, which include, but are not limited to, our products being in the early stage of commercialization and aspects of our technology not having been fully field tested; required maintenance being performed incorrectly or maintenance requirements exceeding our current expectations; failure to deliver the benefits offered by our technology; inability to achieve market acceptance of our products; our warranty obligations and other risks and uncertainties described more fully in the section titled 'Risk Factors' in the Company's Quarterly Report on Form 10-K filed on March 5, 2026, and the Company's other filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Except as required by law, ESS is not undertaking any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-9XG3-RSRS-S4YF-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ KRISTEN BEJARANO JOINS SWINERTON TO EXPAND MISSION CRITICAL AND DATA CENTER CAPABILITIES

Apr. 8, 2026 — Swinerton has appointed Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, adding depth to its leadership in data center infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of experience delivering complex mission critical work for global technology firms. She has launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center division and led Americas data center program strategy and procurement. In the new role she will lead the company's mission critical strategy, working with clients to translate technical requirements into plans that emphasize speed to market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Her background in cooling technology and renewable energy integration is expected to help Swinerton meet rising market demand. Chany, Swinerton's Chief Revenue Officer, welcomed the appointment, highlighting the potential to strengthen client relationships and accelerate growth in the sector. Swinerton remains committed to quality and innovation and to serving projects across the United States as an employee-owned firm dating back to 1888.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP TRIO FOR NORTHEAST DIVISION

Swinerton has announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division, naming Jay Quackenbush as vice president and division manager, effective 16th March 2026, with Nicholas Schifferle appointed director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive. The moves come as the firm intensifies its Northeast growth strategy.Quackenbush, a New Yorker, brings nearly three decades of experience leading complex construction projects and a strong grasp of New York City's building conditions and client networks that support both facility management and ground-up work.Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, offers more than 20 years of international business development experience, including managing design and construction programs across Asia and Australia and founding a San Francisco design-build firm. Murphy, who has overseen more than $650 million in commercial interiors projects, will lead multiple project teams to ensure high quality and client satisfaction, working with long-time division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana.Since its Northeast launch in 2021, the division has delivered a steady stream of office fit-outs, historic renovations and ground-up construction across all five boroughs and is expanding into education, residential and public sectors as the pipeline grows.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP CHANGE AS DON ADAIR STEPS DOWN AND CHRIS CHANY TAKES THE REINS

Swinerton announced that Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, after a nearly 40-year career in the construction industry. The retirement was communicated to employee-owners on June 13, 2025, as part of the leadership transition.Adair began his Swinerton career in 1988 as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles, later leading the expansion of the San Diego office before ascending to Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer in 2019.Chris Chany has been named Adair’s successor. Joining Swinerton Builders in 2013, Chany is known for fostering partnerships and driving strategic growth. He stated that he will maintain the company’s core values and focus on building relationships and delivering strong client experiences and dependable project delivery.The transition aims to sustain momentum for Swinerton in a competitive market and reinforce its standing as a leading player in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN ANALYTICS FOR LARGE-SCALE CONSTRUCTION

Track3D and Hensel Phelps announced a multi-year agreement to extend Track3D's Reality Intelligence across more than 200 active projects, moving beyond a pilot phase as of Apr. 1, 2026.The partnership aims to standardize portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier detection of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex builds while boosting schedule reliability and oversight.A San Francisco International Airport case study demonstrated the platform's proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and involvement of field personnel, reinforcing its value in infrastructure work; a March update introduced an admin dashboard, Groups capability and capture-free floorplan status capture to streamline management.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ELEVATES MICAH CHOY TO LEAD SUPERINTENDENT ON REGIONAL KITCHEN PROJECT

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.In his tenure with Hensel Phelps, Choy has worked on notable projects in Hawaii and Guam across aviation, wastewater, and federal markets.His promotion underscores Hensel Phelps' focus on cultivating talent and positioning Choy to lead the Regional Kitchen initiative, which aims to enhance educational facilities in Hawaii.

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS SIGNAL SCALABLE AI IN CONSTRUCTION WITH 200-PROJECT DEPLOYMENT

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable case, Track3D highlighted a San Francisco International Airport deployment where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes. The company also detailed a March product release that adds an admin dashboard and Groups for centralized portfolio visibility, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans for easier site documentation.Track3D is pursuing its go-to-market strategy with a focus on specialty contractors and plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in its growth as it shifts toward automated production tracking and enhanced margins.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUANTA SERVICES [3 News That Matters]

+ QUANTA SERVICES GROWS PLATFORM WITH TRI-CITY GROUP DEAL, ADDS WILSON CONSTRUCTION CO. AND BILLINGS FLYING SERVICE

Quanta Services completed the Tri-City Group acquisition announced on 6th April 2026, expanding its integrated engineering and construction offerings for electric power and renewables.The Davenport-based Tri-City Group has a long history in the Quad Cities dating to 1895 and rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diversified services. It includes Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, with projects across 46 states and several countries.Lori Rochholz, Tri-City's marketing director, said the brand will remain unchanged and that the Palmer Family will continue to lead, ensuring continuity for customers and the community.Quanta CEO Duke Austin said the acquisition, together with Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service, broadens the crafts-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs nationwide. The company, which employs about 69,000 people, aims to meet growing infrastructure demand.

+ QUANTA EMPHASIZES DISCIPLINE AND PARTNERSHIPS IN 2026 OUTLOOK AT JEFFERIES CONFERENCE

Quanta Services outlined its 2026 strategy at the Jefferies conference on Mar. 3, 2026, signaling optimism after a solid prior year. The leadership stressed opportunities in clean-energy deployment and infrastructure, with plans to bolster operations and broaden offerings. A key focus remains the data-center market and risk-managed partnerships with midcap utilities such as NIPSCO to deliver tailored solutions. The company reaffirmed its commitment to organic growth and disciplined capital allocation, including selective acquisitions to create long-term value.

+ QUANTA SERVICES ALIGNS WITH UTILITIES TO DELIVER LARGE-SCALE ENERGY-TRANSITION PROJECTS ACROSS NORTH AMERICA

Quanta Services has become a central infrastructure platform for utilities, renewables and grid modernization, and its work underpins North America's energy transition. Not a consumer brand, its reach spans power lines, substations, pipelines and fiber networks essential for a more electrified future.The company focuses on engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services, enabling utilities to outsource complex projects and shrink contractor-management hurdles, a critical capability as networks are upgraded and decarbonized.It operates through three core lines—electric power infrastructure, underground utility and energy infrastructure, and communications infrastructure—allowing it to tackle high-voltage transmission, urban undergrounding and advanced communications networks, aided by LiDAR and grid digitalization.In a market with MasTec and MYR Group, Quanta differentiates through specialization and scale and its ability to deliver large-scale modernization, appealing to utilities investing in grid hardening and renewable integration; investors see a premium valuation indicating confidence in growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ JOINT VENTURE OF BARTON MALOW AND MARCH-WESTIN TO LEAD MILAN PUSKAR STADIUM WEST TOWER

MORGANTOWN, W.Va. - West Virginia University's athletics department will partner with Barton Malow and March-Westin as construction managers for the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project, set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture pairs national and local leadership to guide the renovation.The firms will oversee the project from conception through completion, coordinating preconstruction planning, cost and schedule management, procurement and on-site activities, and serving as the primary liaison among the university, architects and engineers.The collaboration blends March-Westin’s campus experience with Barton Malow’s national stadium renovation background, aiming to minimize disruption to fans while delivering the modernization the project requires.

+ BARTON MALOW TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION OF ALUMNIFI FIELD FOR DETROIT CITY FC

Barton Malow will serve as construction manager for AlumniFi Field, Detroit City FC's 15,000-seat new home on the site of the former Southwest Detroit Hospital, with a planned spring 2027 opening.The engagement was confirmed on 28th April 2026 as the club moves forward in the USL Championship, highlighting the need for a larger venue to meet growing demand.Located between Corktown and Mexicantown, AlumniFi Field is designed as a community hub featuring a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands with canopies, and spaces for community activity and events, in addition to serving as a concert venue.Officials say the project will honor the hospital's legacy, including its commitment to diversity in hiring since 1973, and will reflect the character and authenticity of Detroit.

+ BARTON MALOW, SHERIDAN ANNOUNCE OUTREACH SESSION FOR NEW MACON ARENA

Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction will host an information session for subcontractors and suppliers on the New Macon Arena project, aiming to outline the project scope, schedule, and available trade bid opportunities for SLBE, MBE, WBE, and DBE firms. The event is slated for 24th March 2026, from 11:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. at the Macon Centreplex, 200 Coliseum Drive.Attendees will hear a brief presentation followed by a Q&A and time to network with project leads. Registration is optional but encouraged via the Barton Malow website.OSBA supports the outreach and stresses the importance of local participation; OSBA Director Charise Stephens-Merriweather notes that the size of a project does not come along often, and local businesses should have a competitive bid opportunity from the outset. Mayor Lester Miller adds that the arena is expected to be a significant economic driver for the city.The new Macon Arena will be constructed on the current Macon Coliseum site and is anticipated to cover over 230,000 square feet, with seating for about 8,500 for concerts. It will feature modern amenities and a flexible design suitable for various events, including sports, and Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction are collaborating to deliver a high-quality, community-focused project.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WINS BID TO DELIVER WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT ADJACENT TO KIA CENTER

Turner Construction has been named builder for Westcourt Orlando, the 900,000 to 1,000,000-square-foot mixed-use district near the Kia Center, with enabling work underway as of 22nd April 2026.The project followed an extensive preconstruction phase. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, cited Turner's track record on complex builds, particularly in the Space Coast, as a decisive factor. "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us and build this project," Chapman remarked.Turner has operated in Central Florida since 1984, with an Orlando office and regional leadership under vice president Jeff Justen cited by developers as important factors in the decision.Westcourt includes a 265-key Kimpton hotel, about 269 high-rise residences, 300,000 square feet of Class A office space, 125,000 square feet of retail, a 3,500-capacity events venue, and roughly 1,140 parking stalls. Published timelines show a start in Q1 2026 and a potential finish by late 2028, though mobilization could slip to the end of 2026.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 News That Matters]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION HONORED ON FORTUNE'S 100 BEST LIST FOR 2026

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition highlights PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace and strong employee engagement.It marks the 17th time PCL has earned a spot on the list, with a No. 91 ranking for 2026. The designation is linked to the company's employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Fortune's list is widely viewed as a gauge of organizational health and employee experience, often correlating with stronger business performance.In addition, PCL’s year also featured an Engineering News-Record ranking, placing No. 11 on the Top 400 Contractors list for 2025, underscoring leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while prioritizing people.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION LEADS INFRASTRUCTURE SOLUTIONS FOR COLORADO RIVER WATER SECURITY

On the 26th of March 2026, the Colorado River's drought conditions are tightening supplies across the West.Lake Powell is 162 feet below full and holds about 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a reassessment of basin-wide water management.PCL Construction is pushing for expanded water reuse infrastructure as a central resilience tool, aligning with a projected national average of $47.1 billion in water reuse investments over the next decade. Adoption varies among the seven basin states.With the fall 2026 expiration looming, PCL's Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix provides a model for maintaining reliable supplies amid growth, and participants at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference highlighted the need for a multifaceted approach.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC MERGER UNITES AUDDIA AND THRAMANN AS MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC.

Both boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness and supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is planned as Auddia's four directors will join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO. Thramann will also be subject to a 180-day lock-up on his McCarthy Finney shares. The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and Nasdaq listing requirements. McCarthy Finney aims to pursue AI applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services, leveraging Auddia's technology portfolio.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDS MOMENTUM IN HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION WITH CALIFORNIA TOWER MILESTONE

The California Tower milestone aligns with UC Davis Health's broader Vision 2030, a plan to more than double the campus footprint. Opening is targeted for 2030, finalizing a major expansion that will deliver increased patient capacity and upgraded facilities. In addition to this project, McCarthy recently completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December, underscoring the firm's active role in healthcare construction even as early 2026 market indicators hint at a potential slowdown in some healthcare-project planning.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS SETS INDUSTRY BENCHMARK WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS DISCLOSURE

McCarthy Holdings has released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, issued on 16th February 2026 and developed in collaboration with Gravity. The report maps Scope 1, Scope 2, and key Scope 3 emissions, with the Scope 1 and 2 data independently audited and given limited assurance, demonstrating a commitment to transparency in the construction sector.As an employee-owned firm, McCarthy emphasizes its core values and regulatory readiness, with sustainability anchored in three priorities: fully understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly toward people and communities, and efficiently managing energy use. The company notes that its emissions data inform broader practices, including biodiversity work and responsible fuel management, while its digital tools and AI support smarter project decisions.In addition, McCarthy highlights an advanced PV recycling program for solar panels, partnering with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and promote a circular economy. Through data-driven insights, the firm aims to strengthen communities and set a high standard for responsible construction.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-92P3-RWC8-P4FN-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY HOLDINGS

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC MERGER UNITES AUDDIA AND THRAMANN AS MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC.

Both boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness and supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is planned as Auddia's four directors will join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO. Thramann will also be subject to a 180-day lock-up on his McCarthy Finney shares. The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and Nasdaq listing requirements. McCarthy Finney aims to pursue AI applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services, leveraging Auddia's technology portfolio.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDS MOMENTUM IN HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION WITH CALIFORNIA TOWER MILESTONE

The California Tower milestone aligns with UC Davis Health's broader Vision 2030, a plan to more than double the campus footprint. Opening is targeted for 2030, finalizing a major expansion that will deliver increased patient capacity and upgraded facilities. In addition to this project, McCarthy recently completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December, underscoring the firm's active role in healthcare construction even as early 2026 market indicators hint at a potential slowdown in some healthcare-project planning.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS SETS INDUSTRY BENCHMARK WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS DISCLOSURE

McCarthy Holdings has released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, issued on 16th February 2026 and developed in collaboration with Gravity. The report maps Scope 1, Scope 2, and key Scope 3 emissions, with the Scope 1 and 2 data independently audited and given limited assurance, demonstrating a commitment to transparency in the construction sector.As an employee-owned firm, McCarthy emphasizes its core values and regulatory readiness, with sustainability anchored in three priorities: fully understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly toward people and communities, and efficiently managing energy use. The company notes that its emissions data inform broader practices, including biodiversity work and responsible fuel management, while its digital tools and AI support smarter project decisions.In addition, McCarthy highlights an advanced PV recycling program for solar panels, partnering with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and promote a circular economy. Through data-driven insights, the firm aims to strengthen communities and set a high standard for responsible construction.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN ANALYTICS FOR LARGE-SCALE CONSTRUCTION

Track3D and Hensel Phelps announced a multi-year agreement to extend Track3D's Reality Intelligence across more than 200 active projects, moving beyond a pilot phase as of Apr. 1, 2026.The partnership aims to standardize portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier detection of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex builds while boosting schedule reliability and oversight.A San Francisco International Airport case study demonstrated the platform's proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and involvement of field personnel, reinforcing its value in infrastructure work; a March update introduced an admin dashboard, Groups capability and capture-free floorplan status capture to streamline management.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ELEVATES MICAH CHOY TO LEAD SUPERINTENDENT ON REGIONAL KITCHEN PROJECT

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.In his tenure with Hensel Phelps, Choy has worked on notable projects in Hawaii and Guam across aviation, wastewater, and federal markets.His promotion underscores Hensel Phelps' focus on cultivating talent and positioning Choy to lead the Regional Kitchen initiative, which aims to enhance educational facilities in Hawaii.

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS SIGNAL SCALABLE AI IN CONSTRUCTION WITH 200-PROJECT DEPLOYMENT

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable case, Track3D highlighted a San Francisco International Airport deployment where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes. The company also detailed a March product release that adds an admin dashboard and Groups for centralized portfolio visibility, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans for easier site documentation.Track3D is pursuing its go-to-market strategy with a focus on specialty contractors and plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in its growth as it shifts toward automated production tracking and enhanced margins.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression.\n\nThe decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, \"If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.\"\n\nTurner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 News That Matters]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION HONORED ON FORTUNE'S 100 BEST LIST FOR 2026

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition highlights PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace and strong employee engagement.It marks the 17th time PCL has earned a spot on the list, with a No. 91 ranking for 2026. The designation is linked to the company's employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Fortune's list is widely viewed as a gauge of organizational health and employee experience, often correlating with stronger business performance.In addition, PCL’s year also featured an Engineering News-Record ranking, placing No. 11 on the Top 400 Contractors list for 2025, underscoring leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while prioritizing people.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION LEADS INFRASTRUCTURE SOLUTIONS FOR COLORADO RIVER WATER SECURITY

On the 26th of March 2026, the Colorado River's drought conditions are tightening supplies across the West.Lake Powell is 162 feet below full and holds about 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a reassessment of basin-wide water management.PCL Construction is pushing for expanded water reuse infrastructure as a central resilience tool, aligning with a projected national average of $47.1 billion in water reuse investments over the next decade. Adoption varies among the seven basin states.With the fall 2026 expiration looming, PCL's Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix provides a model for maintaining reliable supplies amid growth, and participants at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference highlighted the need for a multifaceted approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE TO INVEST $64.6 MILLION IN BIRMINGHAM HQ UPGRADE BACKED BY COUNTY GRANT

On 25th March 2026, the Jefferson County Commission approved a jobs grant to Brasfield & Gorrie to back a Birmingham expansion that includes a new office tower, a parking deck expansion, a training and event center, and a wellness center.The expansion involves a total investment of $64,584,500 and would create 80 new jobs with an hourly wage of $39.51, supported by a county grant capped at $240,000.Brasfield & Gorrie’s plan includes land purchase of $2,884,500, construction at $56,100,000, and equipment at $5,600,000. The program also calls for a return of $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund.

+ BIRMINGHAM HQ EXPANSION PLAN: FOUR-STORY CAMPUS BUILDING, PARKING DECK UPGRADE, AND LOCAL JOBS

Brasfield &Gorrie unveiled its plan to expand its Birmingham national headquarters, presenting it to the city council committee on 10th March 2026, as part of a long-term growth strategy for the city.The project would add a four-story, roughly 80,000-square-foot building on the HQ campus along 7th Avenue South, with the first floor serving as an event and training center featuring a banquet hall, an auditorium, and training rooms.The upper floors would be devoted to office uses to support national operations, and a fourth level would be added to the existing parking deck to provide about 125 more spaces.The deal contemplates 80 new Birmingham-based full-time jobs with an average salary around $82,000; authorities would provide incentives up to $80,000; completion possible by late 2027 if approved.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE ELEVATES MIKE FOUSHEE TO HEAD CAROLINAS OPERATIONS

Brasfield & Gorrie has named Mike Foushee to lead the Carolinas operations, with the appointment taking effect from 10th February 2026. He will be based in the Charlotte office and oversee strategy and operations for the Raleigh and Greenville offices.A Greenville native, Foushee has spent more than two decades with Brasfield & Gorrie advancing through operational leadership roles, most recently as regional president in Atlanta where he directed teams on large projects such as Science Square at Georgia Tech and Omni Hotels developments.Foushee will prioritize expanding Brasfield & Gorrie’s presence across commercial, healthcare and industrial sectors in the Carolinas, backed by the firm’s turnkey delivery model that covers site selection, in-house design, procurement and MEP management to accelerate schedules and mitigate risk.The Carolinas portfolio includes landmark projects like Ally Charlotte Center and the Carroll A. Campbell Jr. U.S. Courthouse in Greenville, and Foushee will also continue the company’s community investment and workforce development initiatives in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement With State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-V303-RWJ9-W1PM-00000-00Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement With State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisVermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement With State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

IRVINE, CA, May 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: CETY) (“CETY” or the“Company”), a clean energy technology and solutions provider focused on converting waste and heat into power and fuels, today announced execution by its affiliate, Vermont Renewable Gas (VRG), of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Vermont Agency of Natural Resources, marking a significant milestone in the development of VRG's proposed 2.2 MW renewable energy facility in Lyndon, Vermont. The MOU, filed with the Vermont Public Utility Commission, reflects a comprehensive agreement on environmental and regulatory matters between VRG and Vermont's primary environmental oversight agency and establishes a clear framework for project approval and ongoing compliance. A Project Aligned with Vermont's Environmental Standards
Vermont has long been a pioneer and leading force in environmental sustainability action, and initiatives for local production of clean energy Under the terms of the MOU, the Project will proceed subject to rigorous, enforceable conditions, including:


Full compliance with all air quality, water, and stormwater permits

Ongoing regulatory oversight and inspection by state agencies

Periodic testing of biochar for environmental safety, including heavy metals and emerging contaminants, in coordination with the Vermont Agency of Agriculture, Food and Markets

Sustainable sourcing of wood-based feedstock under an approved forestry management plan

The Agency of Natural Resources has concluded that, subject to satisfying these conditions, the Project will not result in an adverse environmental impact, nor increased greenhouse gas emissions.
Addressing Community Priorities: Transparency, Safety, and Accountability
CETY recognizes and respects the concerns raised by community members during VRG's public engagement process, particularly related to emissions, site operations, and project transparency.
In response, CETY is committed to:

Project transparency, including providing detailed information on comparable operating facilities, with project data made accessible to VRG's stakeholders

Operational accountability, through supporting VRG's commitment to continuous monitoring, reporting, and third-party verification

Community engagement, together with VRG, ensuring ongoing communication with local stakeholders throughout development and operations

“This agreement represents an important step forward in this clean energy initiative, and reinforces our commitment to operating with full transparency and accountability,” said Kam Mahdi.“Both CETY and VRG are dedicated to providing the level of visibility and access that the community and decision-makers expect, on an ongoing basis throughout the life of the project.”
A Critical Step Toward Project Approval
The MOU is expected to be incorporated into the Project's Certificate of Public Good (CPG) proceeding before the Public Utility Commission and serves as a foundational element supporting final regulatory approval.
About the Project
The VRG facility is designed to convert locally sourced biomass and agricultural residuals into renewable energy, supporting:

Local energy generation and distribution

Reduction of waste streams

Long-term sustainable resource utilization

The Project is being developed with a focus on environmental stewardship, operational safety, and long-term community benefit.
About Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY)
Headquartered in Irvine, California, Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY) is a rising leader in the zero-emission revolution by offering eco-friendly green energy solutions, clean energy fuels and alternative electric power for small and mid-sized projects in North America, Europe, and Asia. CETY also holds a minority ownership interest in, and is affiliated with Vermont renewable Gas LLC. We deliver power from heat and biomass with zero emission and low cost. The Company's principal products are Waste Heat Recovery Solutions using our patented Clean CycleTM generator to create electricity. Waste to Energy Solutions convert waste products created in manufacturing, agriculture, wastewater treatment plants and other industries to electricity and BioChar. Engineering, Consulting and Project Management Solutions provide expertise and experience in developing clean energy projects for municipal and industrial customers and Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) companies.
CETY's common stock is currently traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol“CETY.” For more information, visit .
Follow CETY on our social media channels: Twitter | LinkedIn | Facebook
About Vermont Renewable Gas
Vermont Renewable Gas, LLC is a developer of renewable energy infrastructure focused on converting underutilized organic resources into sustainable power solutions. The Company is committed to advancing projects that meet stringent environmental standards while supporting local economies and energy resilience.
